prompts.subject: the White Tree of Gondor in the Court of the Fountain
prompts.prompt: Subject: the White Tree of Gondor in the Court of the Fountain. Description: a young white tree IN FULL LEAF AND FLOWER — the slender trunk and boughs of smooth white-silver bark densely clothed in dark green leaves, and among them many small white blossoms open on the branches; healthy, verdant and blooming, NOT bare, NOT leafless, NOT a dead or winter tree, NOT a bare stick. A hand-drawn BOTANICAL study plate, traditional pen-and-ink with watercolour washes on warm off-white cream paper, in the manner of a nineteenth-century naturalist's folio plate. Thin confident dark sepia-brown pen linework with fine hand-inked cross-hatching; obsessive surface detail. Colour laid on like real watercolour — soft, muted, slightly desaturated, streaking and pooling unevenly, paper texture showing through; never a flat digital fill. A short handwritten flowing calligraphic ink caption near the foot of the page. Looks like traditional media on paper — NOT vector art, NOT a cel-shaded cartoon, NOT a 3D render, NOT a photograph, NO gradients, NO glow, NO thick uniform outlines, NO frame or border. A SINGLE plant specimen centred on an otherwise empty warm-white page, every vein and stamen drawn individually, with one or two small numbered detail studies beside it — a blossom enlarged, a leaf, a seed — as a botanist would set them out. NO landscape, NO figures. The caption is the two or three words "The White Tree of Gondor" — write no other words on the page.

Looks like traditional media on paper — NOT vector art, NOT a cel-shaded cartoon, NOT a 3D render, NOT a photograph, NO gradients, NO glow, NO thick uniform outlines, NO frame or border. A SINGLE animal, whole and fully visible in profile or three-quarter view, centred on an otherwise empty warm-white page with only the faintest suggestion of ground beneath it. Anatomy coherent and believable, drawn from observation as a naturalist would record a specimen — musculature, the lie of fur or feather or scale, the set of the limbs. One or two small numbered detail studies beside it: a head, a foot, a wing, a tooth. NO landscape, NO human figures, NO rider, NO action scene. The caption is the two or three words "The Moths of Fangorn" — write no other words on the page.

prompts.subject: a purple butterfly of the Mirkwood canopy
prompts.prompt: Subject: a purple butterfly of the Mirkwood canopy. Description: a large butterfly with wings of deep velvet purple shot with darker bands, drawn spread and again folded in profile; the kind that loves the tops of the oak-wood, seen only above the black roof of the forest. A hand-drawn ZOOLOGICAL study plate, traditional pen-and-ink with watercolour washes on warm off-white cream paper, in the manner of a nineteenth-century naturalist's folio plate. Thin confident dark sepia-brown pen linework with fine hand-inked cross-hatching; obsessive surface detail. Colour laid on like real watercolour — soft, muted, slightly desaturated, streaking and pooling unevenly, paper texture showing through; never a flat digital fill. A short handwritten flowing calligraphic ink caption near the foot of the page. Looks like traditional media on paper — NOT vector art, NOT a cel-shaded cartoon, NOT a 3D render, NOT a photograph, NO gradients, NO glow, NO thick uniform outlines, NO frame or border. A SINGLE animal, whole and fully visible in profile or three-quarter view, centred on an otherwise empty warm-white page with only the faintest suggestion of ground beneath it. Anatomy coherent and believable, drawn from observation as a naturalist would record a specimen — musculature, the lie of fur or feather or scale, the set of the limbs. One or two small numbered detail studies beside it: a head, a foot, a wing, a tooth. NO landscape, NO human figures, NO rider, NO action scene. The caption is the two or three words "The Butterflies of Mirkwood" — write no other words on the page.
